name: Madison-Grant United School Corporation
description:
Madison-Grant is committed to creating a safe atmosphere and productive learning environment in each of their schools. They continually raise the bar of expectation for themselves and students, because they believe—in the words attributed to American inventor Charles Kettering—that “high achievement always takes place in the framework of high expectation.” They find deep satisfaction in providing an educational support system that emphasizes self-discipline and accountability and that inspires our students to stretch and grow.

Madison-Grant schools offer a variety of programs that cater to students’ unique needs and various learning styles. They have special education and high ability programs, all-day kindergarten and preschool programs. A well-rounded list of options for both in-school and extracurricular opportunities is available, including athletics, fine arts, technology, AP classes, New Teacher Academy, and much more.

name: Wabash City Schools
description:
Wabash City Schools serves roughly 1500 students in the county seat of Wabash, Indiana.  Wabash, a town of 10,500, is located 45 minutes southwest of Fort Wayne and 2 hours north of Indianapolis.  The district is comprised of 4 buildings: L. H. Carpenter Early Learning Center, O.J. Neighbours Elementary, Wabash Middle School, and Wabash High School (Wabash University High School).

Wabash City Schools is committed to the motto The Apache Way: The Right Way, Every Day.  We believe in applying this commitment in all of our classrooms, in our finances, and in our community partnerships.
